title: Chrysanthemum carneum
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum carneum is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Chrysanthemum, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia. Chrysanthemum carneum is classified as a perennial herb, with a specific classification of Chrysanthemum carneum (Hemsl.) Kitam. Further, it is placed in the family Asteraceae, subfamily Asteroideae, and tribe Anthemideae. This classification helps in understanding the plant's relationships and evolutionary history.
uses: Chrysanthemum carneum has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese traditional medicine. Its flowers and leaves are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The plant is also used to aid in the treatment of respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, it has been used to promote sweating and relieve headaches. However, its medicinal uses may vary depending on the specific application and cultural context.
benefits: Chrysanthemum carneum is known to have various health benefits, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce fever. It is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach pain. In addition, Chrysanthemum carneum is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Chrysanthemum carneum for these purposes.

constituents: Chrysanthemum carneum contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties and are often used in traditional medicine. intro: The Chrysanthemum carneum, also known as the Yellow Chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant in the Asteraceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throats. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum carneum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chrysanthemum carneum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to soups and other culinary preparations.
